chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI. Date Order with signature of Judge Cr. Bail Appl No.1233 of 2020 Sajid Iqbal & others....................Vs................................... The State Cr. Bail Appl No.1234 of 2020 Sajid Iqbal............................... Vs................................... The State Cr. Bail Appl No.1235 of 2020 Habib-ur-Rehman...................... Vs................................... The State For hearing of Bail Application. 11.09.2020. Mr. Khair Muhammad, advocate for applicants Mr. Ali Haider Salim, DPG a/w PI/IO, Riaz Ali Qureshi O R D E R Applicants were arrested on 28.05.2020 at 2240 hours from General Tyre Road, opposite Olumpia Textile Mills, Karachi by police of Police Station Quaidabad, Karachi after an encounter, and from them one unlicensed 30 bore TT pistol each was recovered. As a result they have booked in the present cases. Learned defence counsel has pleaded for their bail stating that investigation has been completed and applicants are no more required for further enquiry. Applicability of section 324 PPC requires for further enquiry as no one from either party was injured. These contentions have not been rebutted by learned DPG, who has however opposed grant of bail to applicants on the ground that there is one more case each against them besides the present cases. 3. We are of the view that applicants are entitled to grant of bail as investigation has been concluded and they are no more required for further enquiry. No one was injured in the alleged encounter, therefore culpability of applicants u/s 324 PPC requires further enquiry. The other case was registered only after registration of present cases would not improve merits of these cases.

No one was injured in the alleged encounter, therefore culpability of applicants u/s 324 PPC requires further enquiry. The other case was registered only after registration of present cases would not improve merits of these cases. Therefore, we allow these applications, and grant bail to the applicants subject to their furnishing one solvent surety in each case in the sum of Rs.100,000/ (Rupees one hundred thousand only) with P.R. bonds in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 2 4. These bail applications are disposed of in the above terms. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. Judge Judge Rafiq P.A.
